name: B.S. in Computer Science

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The plan of study progresses cleanly across 8 terms from CS 100 (Roadmap) to CS 113/114 introductory sequence, into theory (CS 241, CS 341), systems (CS 332, CS 350, CS 356), and a CS 490 -> CS 491 capstone, with math prerequisites (MATH 111/112/333/337) sequenced to support them; deepestPrereqChain of 6 reflects genuine layered build-up rather than a flat list.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish named elective tracks (e.g., AI/data, security, systems) so the four '300+ CS Elective' slots map to coherent specialization pathways instead of undifferentiated choices.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: Prerequisite chains are well-formed and consistently gated (e.g., CS 113 -> CS 114 -> CS 241/280, each 'with a grade C or better'), and a deepestPrereqChain of 6 is sensible for a 120-credit BS; however missingCourseCount is 6, with dangling references including PHYS 111A, PHYS 121A, YWCC 207, and YWCC 307 (courses that appear in the plan but resolve to no catalog entry) plus GER 200/GER 300 placeholder references.
dimensions.suggestion: Add or link catalog entries for the lab and 1-credit YWCC courses (PHYS 111A/121A, YWCC 207/307) and standardize the GER 200/300 placeholder labels so no referenced code resolves to nothing.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives appear anywhere in the provided data; only a term-by-term course list and per-course descriptions are present, so a reader cannot see what graduates are expected to know or be able to do.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish an explicit set of program learning outcomes / educational objectives on the catalog page (e.g., ABET-style student outcomes) so competencies are stated rather than inferred from course titles.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: creditReconcileDelta is 0.0 and summedTermCredits (120) exactly matches statedTotalCredits (120), with per-term loads tightly balanced between 14 and 16 credits and a visible mix of major core, math/science, GER, and free electives.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface a one-line credit-category summary (major core vs. GER vs. electives) on the page so the already-balanced distribution is transparent to prospective students at a glance.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Terms are clearly labeled and per-course descriptions are informative, but the parsed page contains several broken rows: stray URL fragments as course titles ('requirements/natural-science-ger/)', 'general-education-requirements/ger-200-level/)') and orphaned page-break text ('230 B.S. in Computer Science and B.S. in Applied Physics'), plus null-code/null-credit line noise that fragments the plan of study.
dimensions.suggestion: Clean the plan-of-study parsing so GER/elective placeholders render as single labeled rows and remove leaked URL fragments and page-break headers from the item list.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Required and elective courses reflect current practice: CS 301 Introduction to Data Science, CS 351 Introduction to Cybersecurity, CS 356 Computer Networks, CS 331 Database System Design, CS 288 Intensive Programming in Linux (Php/Python/C, Apache/MySQL), and CS 490 software engineering plus a team-based CS 491 senior project, alongside professional-development YWCC 207/307.
dimensions.suggestion: Add an explicit required or clearly-flagged machine learning / AI course to complement the data-science introduction and keep pace with current hiring demand.

descriptionQuality.justification: Every sampled course carries a present, specific, and substantive description (e.g., CS 241 on algorithm correctness and complexity, CS 350 on Von Neumann architecture and numeric representation, CS 356 on layered network architecture and routing); none are thin or placeholder, with only expected truncation of excerpts.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are stated unambiguously with consistent grade conditions ('with a grade C or better') and clear boolean logic (e.g., CS 341 '(CS 241 or MATH 226) and CS 280'), but clarity is dented by GER placeholder references in prose (IS 350's 'GER (CS 100, CS 101...)' and 'History and Humanities GER 200 level') and the dangling GER 200/GER 300 codes flagged in metrics.

topSuggestions: Publish explicit program-level learning outcomes / educational objectives on the catalog page, which are currently entirely absent.

Fix the plan-of-study parsing to remove leaked URL fragments and page-break text (e.g., 'requirements/natural-science-ger/)', '230 B.S. in Computer Science and B.S. in Applied Physics') and render each GER/elective placeholder as one clean labeled row.
Resolve the 6 dangling course references by adding or linking catalog entries for PHYS 111A, PHYS 121A, YWCC 207, and YWCC 307 and standardizing the GER 200/GER 300 placeholder labels.
Define named CS elective tracks so the four '300+ CS Elective' slots guide students into coherent specializations.
Add an explicitly required AI/machine-learning course to complement CS 301 Data Science and reflect current industry demand.
